title: Macros 3
description: Macros can use repetition patterns with `$(...)*` to accept variable numbers of arguments.
instructions: Fix the macro that uses repetition patterns.
hints: The repetition in the output must match the repetition in the pattern.
The pattern uses `*` for zero or more repetitions.
Add `*` after the closing parenthesis in the body: $( println!(...); )*
explanation: Macro repetition patterns use `$(...)*` for zero or more, `$(...)+` for one or more, and `$(...)?` for zero or one. In the pattern `$($val:expr),*`, the comma is a separator between matches. In the expansion `$( println!(...); )*`, the code inside is repeated for each match. This is how macros like `vec!` work internally.
